title: Release versions tag current valid premises
description: DECISION: Each Validatorium release references versioned assets and tags premises that are valid and current at that release. Eligibility checks versioned ontology terms, replacement UUID mappings, expiry/TTL, and recurrence. An ephemeral recurring premise can be current in each applicable window and inactive between windows without becoming obsolete; obsolescence is reserved for supersession.
close_reason: Recorded release-relative premise tagging, versioned artifact references, ontology remapping checks, and optional expiry/TTL evaluation in the data model.

title: Tag premises with versioned ontology terms
description: DECISION: Premises are tagged with versioned terms from versioned ontologies. A superseded term-mapping record is marked obsolete and stores reference pointers to its replacement record UUIDs; the obsolete record remains addressable through IPFS.
close_reason: Recorded the concise IPFS versioning rule in the ontology specifications.

title: Storage spike: embedded Kubo + go-orbit-db, two-node replication test
description: THE de-risk (owner: 'spec'd AND tested before any other work'). Stand up an embedded Kubo core node (core.NewNode w/ custom repo, the Berty-proven path), attach go-orbit-db via coreiface.CoreAPI, open a Documents store keyed by UUID. Test: node A writes a micropublication record, node B replicates it over pubsub; then partition and prove reconvergence. Success = two in-process nodes converge on the same record set. Directly attacks the go-orbit-db lag risk.
close_reason: Completed the storage spike: stood up two in-process embedded Kubo nodes, integrated go-orbit-db Document store, proved successful CRUD, automated pubsub replication, and partition recovery/reconvergence. All tests green.

title: Go module scaffold + toolchain
description: Stand up the Go module: go.mod (go 1.26+), cmd/val CLI entrypoint, internal/ layout (storage, orbit, bbolt, ipfsnode). Pin berty.tech/go-orbit-db v1.22.2 and its Kubo dep. Prove 'go build' + 'go test ./...' green on an empty skeleton. Foundational — unblocks everything else in this epic.
close_reason: Scaffolded cmd/val and internal packages (storage, orbit, bbolt, ipfsnode), pinned berty.tech/go-orbit-db and verified go build/test green.

title: Premise identity = UUID at creation (not content-hash)
description: DECISION: UUID/accession assigned at creation. Statement string may be refined under the SAME id; premises may split (obsolete + redirect to successors) or be obsoleted. Versioning/pointers ride on IPFS-native mechanisms. RATIONALE (owner): 'the nature of the premise will not change'; content hashes are per-version integrity at most. OVERTURNS: data-model.md SHA-256-of-statement identity.
close_reason: Decision recorded with rationale in issue description. Implementation tracked in separate issues.

title: Single Go binary owns the global tier
description: DECISION: One Go process = CLI + bbolt + embedded IPFS (Kubo) + go-orbit-db; Node.js only for Argdown parse/generate. RATIONALE: matches Go-CLI-first architecture; go-orbit-db NewOrbitDB takes a Kubo coreiface.CoreAPI and Berty runs this in production (verified). RISK: go-orbit-db ports the pre-2024 orbit-db; encryption/access-controller/identity parity with JS v2-v4 is UNVERIFIED — settled by test, not doc.
close_reason: Decision recorded with rationale in issue description. Implementation tracked in separate issues.

title: Local DB engine is bbolt (not SQLite/Badger/Pebble)
description: DECISION: bbolt for the tier-1 working store. RATIONALE (owner, verbatim): 'I NEVER would have specified sqlite as performant for this purpose; I believe I said bbolt.' The agent-written specs were not authorized.
close_reason: Decision recorded with rationale in issue description. Implementation tracked in separate issues.

title: Two-tier storage: bbolt working store + IPFS/go-orbit-db global store
description: DECISION: Tier 1 embedded performant Go-native working DB (bbolt); Tier 2 distributed global IPFS/OrbitDB store; locally-validated records promoted to global. RATIONALE (owner): global-community nature makes IPFS/orbitdb natural; premise records relatively stable long-term. ALTERNATIVES REJECTED: agent-written specs' Badger/Pebble+SQLite (SQLite explicitly rejected as not performant); storage-architecture.md custom-libp2p protocol.
close_reason: Decision recorded with rationale in issue description. Implementation tracked in separate issues.

title: Two-tier storage layer: bbolt working store + IPFS/go-orbit-db global store
description: Priority milestone. Owner directive: this must be spec'd AND tested before any other project work. Tier 1 = embedded, performant, Go-native working/cache DB (bbolt). Tier 2 = distributed global datastore on IPFS via embedded Kubo node + go-orbit-db. Single Go binary owns both; Node.js only for Argdown. Verified substrate: go-orbit-db NewOrbitDB takes a Kubo coreiface.CoreAPI; Berty runs this in production.

title: Align public documentation with evidence-validation model
description: Audit project-owned README and specification files. Remove debate, consensus, voting, and social-adjudication framing where it conflicts with evidence-based factual validation. Preserve formal Micropublication support/challenge relationships. Capture release-relative validity, versioned ontology terms, expiry/TTL and recurrence, runtime/JIT re-validation, and instrument-generated evidence without overstating unimplemented features.
close_reason: Aligned public documentation and architecture specs with evidence-derived validity, UUID/CID versioning, exact releases, temporal/runtime re-validation, and higher-level automated experimentation; removed social-status and human-acceptance validity models; added durable Beads memories; all tests/build/vet pass.

title: Fix pre-push repository hygiene findings
Two-tier storage design doc (grounded in decisions + spike results)
description: Write the storage design doc reflecting ONLY owner-approved decisions + verified research + spike/parity findings. Supersede storage-architecture.md's custom-libp2p+SQLite track and data-model.md content-hash identity. This is the 'spec'd' half; the spike is the 'tested' half. Finalize AFTER the spike so the doc rests on evidence, not assumption.

title: Tier-boundary storage abstraction
description: Define the interface between tier-1 (bbolt working) and tier-2 (global). Global backend behind an interface so go-orbit-db is the implementation but the custom-libp2p fallback (or a Node OrbitDB sidecar) could swap in without redesign. Encodes the promotion seam.

title: bbolt working store: schema + Store interface
description: Tier-1 working DB. Define the Store interface (Put/Get/List/Delete over micropublication drafts + validation-in-progress + materialized pointers), bbolt bucket layout, and UUID keying. Bench basic ops. Separate from the IPFS block datastore (bbolt sits beside IPFS, not underneath). Serves accessibility+offline-first: full local operation with zero connectivity.

title: Verify go-orbit-db feature parity vs JS v2-v4
description: go-orbit-db ports pre-2024 orbit-db. Empirically determine which of these the Go port actually has: modular payload/replication ENCRYPTION, current Access Controllers, Identities module, Documents/KeyValue/EventLog stores. Test each capability that the private/shared-namespace and signing epics depend on. Output: a capability matrix + gap list feeding the storage design doc.

title: Identity is attribution-only; GPG signing; anonymized; no klout
description: DECISION: identity tracked ONLY for attribution; premises stand on evidence alone. GPG signature from author's key attributes actions. Attribution anonymized (pseudonymous, not real-world identity). No klout/credit/reputation. PURPOSE: remediation — if an individual OR node corrupts data, enumerate & flag every record they touched. RATIONALE (owner). Supersedes agent-written did:key/Ed25519 self-gen scheme (unconfirmed).

title: Local vs global premises; sensitive/regional via compile-time cv tags
description: DECISION: local premises permitted only if they stay local AND do not conflict with any global premise on a matter of fact. 'sensitive' = public but content-flagged (e.g. not child-suitable). local/global/sensitive/regional handled by cv/ontology TAGS in record annotations + optional indices — not separate subsystems. All vocabularies/ontologies/cv-tables/data-structures FIXED AT COMPILE TIME, author-controlled. RATIONALE (owner).

title: Per-domain namespaced databases; UUID records + pointer namespaces
description: DECISION: per-domain DBs with owner-controlled namespaces (namespace:subdomain:...). Records are UUID-keyed; namespaces are a pointer layer — same premise may be referenced by multiple namespaced pointers. Core namespaces ship with the software (owner controls STRUCTURE, not truth). Private/shared namespaces ride on native IPFS mechanisms. RATIONALE (owner).

title: Automatic publication on validity
description: DECISION: software mechanically enforces validity rules; any NEW premise meeting minimum validity auto-publishes — no separate push step (the node runs IPFS, so local DB writing to the IPFS store IS replication). RATIONALE (owner). Accessibility invariant: a child can state a premise and see if it exists+valid or launch a validation task.

title: Leverage Micropublications ontology + semantic-web standards
description: DECISION: reuse the Micropublications OWL2 ontology (purl.org/mp, MIT) and lean on RDF/SPARQL and proper semantic-web standards throughout rather than reinventing. RATIONALE (owner): 'let's leverage as much proper semantic web standards as we can. SPARQL, RDF, etc.'

comments.author: brett
comments.text: OWNER (emphatic): USE THE MICROPUBLICATION ONTOLOGY (http://purl.org/mp). The model uses MP's claim/evidence/attribution structure; do NOT invent a parallel schema. Status vocabulary (validated/invalidated=proven/disproven, current/obsolete) grades over MP.

comments.author: brett
comments.text: MP model read from source (PMC4530550): a Claim is the principal truth-bearing Statement of a Micropublication. Grounding is STRUCTURAL, not a status label -- a Claim is grounded by a support-graph DAG rooted at the Claim; empirical evidence = Data (tables/images/measurements) + Method (reproducible recipe) + Material; minimal support = Attribution to an Agent. Dispute = challenges/hasChallengeGraphElement (structural). MP defines NO status/validity field.

comments.author: brett
comments.text: MP support vocabulary, verbatim from mp.owl (verified): dataSupports/supportedByData, evidenceSupports/supportedByEvidence, methodSupports/supportedByMethod, supports/supportedBy, hasSupportGraphElement/supportGraphElementOf; challenge side: challenges/challengedBy, hasChallengeGraphElement. 'refute' NOT present (not a science term). => evidence layer uses these MP properties; the invented 6-value evidence-type enum in data-model.md (empirical_observation/replication/model_prediction/historical_record/civil_record/expert_testimony) is software-invented and to be replaced with MP (Data/Method/Material) -- and, if finer evidence typing is wanted, the ECO standard (verify, do not assert).

title: Micropublication core model + full lexical grounding
description: DECISION: record = statement + validity grade + supporting citations, each with own UUID/accession/ontology annotation; no arguments/talk-pages/social layer in the record. EVERY word in the statement grounded to a domain jargon dictionary/ontology OR standard language definition (stricter than data-model.md). Software proposes annotation+provenance under human curation. RATIONALE (owner): micropublications model; 'if someone did all that work already, we should implement it.'

comments.author: brett
comments.text: OWNER (verbatim): "Each premise by definition must have a 'minimal string representation' that serves as the statement of the premise; eg: 'sky is blue'". => The premise's statement IS its minimal string representation — the canonical shortest form. This is the 'shorthand / short string' referenced by the text-generation feature (validatorium-3xe).

comments.author: brett
comments.text: OWNER correctness point: '\''apples are red'\'' is NOT a true premise (universal reading, false — overgeneralization the scope rules reject). The valid minimal form is '\''apples can be red'\''. => The minimal string representation must be the shortest form that is STILL a valid premise; brevity must not cost correctness. Good: '\''sky is blue'\'', '\''apples can be red'\''. Invalid: '\''apples are red'\''.

comments.author: brett
comments.text: Shorthand note (owner) -- do not over-engineer: the minimal string representation could be encoded efficiently using formal-logic symbols. Recorded as a possibility; not a blocker, not to be fixated on.

comments.author: brett
comments.text: IMPLEMENTED: internal/model/model.go — micropublication record built from docs/specs/mp.owl. Every field maps to an MP class/property (documented in comments): Claim=MP:Claim (principal Statement), statement text=MP:statement (the minimal string), evidence=MP:Data/Method/Material via supportedByData/Method/Evidence collected in a support graph (MP:hasSupportGraphElement), challenge graph=MP:hasChallengeGraphElement, citations=MP:Reference/citation, term grounding=MP:SemanticQualifier/semtags, attribution=MP:Attribution/attributedTo/atTime. NO validated/invalidated status (system stores only valid premises). UUID identity + current/obsolete versioning flagged as project layer (MP has neither). Serializes to the _id-keyed go-orbit-db Documents map the spike uses. gofmt/vet clean; 4 tests pass (New/UUID-uniqueness/admission-gate/document-round-trip); whole repo builds.

title: Integrations & standards
description: Leverage existing standards/systems: Micropublications OWL2 ontology (purl.org/mp); microPublication Biology journal conventions (CRediT roles, DOIs, structured metadata); Wikidata/SPARQL for term grounding; truth.dataglut.org integration (Schema.org JSON-LD + HTTP API — owner: real integration target). Prefer RDF/SPARQL/semantic-web standards throughout.

title: Identity, signing & accountability
description: Identity tracked ONLY for attribution — premises stand on evidence alone; identity carries no validity weight. GPG signature from author's key attributes actions (authored premise / attached evidence / flagged claim). Attribution is anonymized (consistent pseudonymous signer, not real-world identity). No klout/credit/reputation/leaderboards. PURPOSE: remediation — if an individual OR node is found corrupting data, enumerate & flag every record they touched. Minimal OpenID login for PORTABLE preferences/config stored in the distributed DB (login from any instance; age-gates sensitive content).

title: Namespaces & governance
description: Per-domain databases with owner-controlled namespaces (form namespace:subdomain:...). UUID-keyed records; namespaces are a POINTER layer (one record may be referenced by multiple namespaced pointers). Core validatorium namespaces ship with the software (author/engineer controls STRUCTURE, not truth). Users add private/shared namespaces (ride on native IPFS mechanisms). Local vs global premises (local must stay local + not conflict with global on fact). Sensitive/regional handled via cv/ontology tags in record annotations + optional indices. All vocabularies/ontologies/cv-tables/structures fixed at COMPILE time.

title: Validation & epistemic-grading engine
description: Mechanical, evidence-based validity determination. Truth determined by evidence alone, universally, zero exceptions — no special/protected/axiomatic premises. Software enforces validity rules; a new premise meeting minimum validity auto-publishes. Accessibility invariant: a child can run the software, state a premise, see whether it already exists+valid, or launch a validation task.

comments.author: brett
comments.text: CORE INVARIANT (owner, emphatic, verbatim): 'ALL PREMISES MUST BE VALID IN THE SYSTEM' / 'THE SYSTEM ONLY STORES VALID PREMISES.' Validity is the admission gate -- invalid candidates are rejected and never stored; there is no stored-invalid-premise state. Ex: 'apples are red' rejected (overgeneralized); 'apples can be red' admitted.

comments.author: brett
comments.text: PREMISE STATUS VOCABULARY (owner): a factual premise is VALIDATED / INVALIDATED (i.e. PROVEN / DISPROVEN by evidence) and CURRENT / OBSOLETE. Facts are proven or disproven, never 'contradicted', never argued. The agent-invented 5-value epistemic enum + argument-based status word are purged from all docs (data-model.md, README.md, evidence-verification.md, participation-model.md, premise-decomposition.md) and replaced by these two axes.

comments.author: brett
comments.text: CONSEQUENCE from MP source: MP has NO status field. 'Proven vs disproven' is represented as presence of supporting vs challenging empirical evidence in the support graph, not a stored word. The validated/invalidated prose in the docs is therefore software-composed/provisional, not MP-grounded, and must be reconciled to MP structure. CURRENT/OBSOLETE = versioning (IPFS layer), not MP.

title: Micropublication data model (RDF-grounded)
description: Core record = a micropublication: (1) statement — one short atomic factual claim; (2) validity grade; (3) supporting citations to empirical evidence. Each object carries its own UUID/accession + ontology annotation. Identity = UUID assigned at creation (NOT content-hash); statement text may be refined under same ID; premises may split (obsolete+redirect) or be obsoleted. Every word in the statement is grounded to a domain jargon dictionary/ontology OR standard language definitions (stricter than agent-written data-model.md). Leverage the Micropublications OWL2 ontology (purl.org/mp, MIT) and semantic-web standards (RDF/SPARQL) rather than reinventing.

title: Rename record well-formedness check to ValidateRecord
description: Rename Micropublication.ValidateStructure to ValidateRecord so the API cannot be confused with factual validity; update tests and direct documentation references.

title: Premise clusters: every equivalent statement of a premise (paraphrase + cross-language)
description:
DECISION (owner): The software GENERATES 'premise clusters'.

DEFINITION (owner, verbatim): "A 'premise cluster' is basically every possible version of stating the same premise (and if we support languages other than English, in every possible language the premise can be stated in)."
=> A cluster = all equivalent human-readable statements of the SAME premise: minimal string representation, formal-logic-encoded form, paraphrases/restatements, and (if multilingual) translations.

ANCHOR (owner, verbatim): "The UUID is the only string you AS SOFTWARE need to concern your ... head about" and "Short strings are for HUMANS."
=> One premise = one UUID = one cluster of equivalent strings. The software addresses premises by UUID only (identity, keys, references, pointers); it never parses a string to determine identity. The short strings are HUMAN-facing surface forms held in the cluster. Refining/adding a statement adds a cluster member without changing the UUID.

title: Auto-publish promotion path (local validity -> IPFS)
description: Enact 'automatic publication on validity': a new premise passing minimum local validation is signed + written into the IPFS-backed store, which IS replication (node runs IPFS). No separate push. Wire the bbolt->global seam. Depends on validation engine (E3) minimum-validity check + the spike.

title: Namespace pointer layer (UUID -> CID)
description: Per-domain namespaced pointer databases (namespace:subdomain:...) over UUID-keyed records via OrbitDB KeyValue/Documents. One record referenceable by multiple namespaced pointers. Core namespaces ship with the binary; private/shared namespaces via native IPFS mechanisms. Blocked by the spike + parity results.

title: truth.dataglut.org is a real integration target
description: DECISION (owner, verbatim): 'this IS NOT a parody site; we will integrate with that site.' Integration surface it advertises: Schema.org JSON-LD, HTTP API endpoints, implementation guide. OPEN: exact integration semantics (consume its verification API / share JSON-LD vocab / emit Validatorium-backed seals) — ground in the actual API before building.

title: Minimal OpenID login for portable preferences/config
description: DECISION: minimally support OpenID login so a user's preferences/config are stored in the distributed DB and available from any running instance (e.g. a child sets age to gate 'sensitive' content). Login is a portable-profile mechanism, NOT an epistemic/validity lever. RATIONALE (owner, verbatim).

title: Spec adjudication & cleanup
description: The 15 agent-written specs in docs/specs were drafted WITHOUT owner sign-off. Owner directive: adjudicate every design decision (mine/acceptable/rejected). Reconcile/supersede: storage-architecture.md custom-libp2p + Badger/Pebble+SQLite; data-model.md content-hash identity; over-narrow scientific scoping. Driven by the validatorium-spec-audit workflow output.

title: Search & retrieval
description: Owner's own design: FM-index / BWT / suffix-array / short-read-aligner-style indexing + MinHash/LSH similarity. This is a SEARCH-layer concern, distinct from storage. Layers on top of the stores.

title: Text generation engine: local model (gemma4) drafts prose from premises + glue text
description:
OWNER (verbatim): "a 'text generation engine' that uses a local model like gemma4 to author draft text based on the premises (and perhaps user-authored glue text as well); the premises could be added using their 'shorthand' / 'short string' representations."

=> Local LLM (gemma4 via Ollama) generates DRAFT text from a set of premises, optionally with user-authored 'glue' text. Premises inserted via their shorthand = the minimal string representation that IS each premise's statement (validatorium-8gv). LLM drafts; human authors/curates.

comments.author: brett
comments.text: RESOLVED (owner): the shorthand/short-string representation = the premise's 'minimal string representation', which by definition serves as the statement of the premise (e.g. 'sky is blue'). Defined at the data-model level — see validatorium-8gv. No separate syntax to design.

title: Argument builder: search premises -> toolkit -> construct arguments (kid-friendly)
description:
OWNER (verbatim): "for kids, they could play with an 'argument builder' where they search for premises, add them to a 'toolkit', and then use the toolkit to construct arguments."

=> Interactive tool: search premises, collect chosen ones into a per-user 'toolkit', then assemble them into arguments. Kid-friendly framing (ties to the accessibility principle: a child can use the system). Product layer; composes with the README 'premises compose into larger arguments' vision and the Argdown module.

title: cytoscape.js graph view of a premise + supporting evidence types
description:
OWNER (verbatim): "a cytoscape.js graphical representation would be perfect for viewing an individual premise, and the supporting evidence types."

=> Render a single premise as a cytoscape.js graph: the premise plus its supporting evidence, distinguished by evidence type (per the micropublication model's evidence types).

title: Web UI: browse/search Validatorium + graphical premise exploration
description:
OWNER (verbatim): "In a polished finished product, users could explore a web view of the 'Validatorium', search for premises; a cytoscape.js graphical representation would be perfect for viewing an individual premise, and the supporting evidence types."

=> Web exploration surface for a finished product: browse/search premises + a cytoscape.js graph of a premise and its supporting evidence types. Future/product layer, in addition to the Go CLI primary interface (README).
